---
title: "Breakout Trading Strategy — Backtest & Analysis"
---

```{=html}
<div id="stats-banner" style="display:flex; justify-content:center; gap:2rem; flex-wrap:wrap; margin:1.5rem 0 2rem;">
  <div class="stat-card">
    <div class="stat-number" id="sn-trades" data-target="0">0</div>
    <div class="stat-label">Total Trades</div>
  </div>
  <div class="stat-card">
    <div class="stat-number" id="sn-winrate" data-target="0" data-suffix="%">0%</div>
    <div class="stat-label">Win Rate</div>
  </div>
  <div class="stat-card">
    <div class="stat-number" id="sn-sharpe" data-target="0" data-decimal="true">0</div>
    <div class="stat-label">Sharpe Ratio</div>
  </div>
  <div class="stat-card">
    <div class="stat-number" id="sn-dd" data-target="0" data-decimal="true" data-suffix="%">0%</div>
    <div class="stat-label">Max Drawdown</div>
  </div>
  <div class="stat-card">
    <div class="stat-number" id="sn-pf" data-target="0" data-decimal="true">0</div>
    <div class="stat-label">Profit Factor</div>
  </div>
</div>

<style>
.stat-card {
  background: rgba(15,23,42,0.9);
  border: 1px solid rgba(0,212,255,0.3);
  border-radius: 16px;
  padding: 1.2rem 2rem;
  text-align: center;
  min-width: 140px;
  transition: all 0.4s ease;
  cursor: default;
  position: relative;
  overflow: hidden;
}
.stat-card::before {
  content: '';
  position: absolute;
  top: -50%; left: -50%;
  width: 200%; height: 200%;
  background: conic-gradient(transparent, rgba(0,212,255,0.1), transparent 30%);
  animation: rotateBorder 4s linear infinite;
}
@keyframes rotateBorder {
  100% { transform: rotate(360deg); }
}
.stat-card:hover {
  border-color: #00d4ff;
  box-shadow: 0 0 30px rgba(0,212,255,0.3), 0 0 60px rgba(0,212,255,0.1);
  transform: translateY(-5px) scale(1.05);
}
.stat-number {
  font-family: 'Orbitron', monospace;
  font-size: 2rem;
  font-weight: 900;
  color: #00d4ff;
  position: relative;
  z-index: 1;
}
.stat-label {
  font-family: 'Rajdhani', sans-serif;
  color: #94a3b8;
  font-size: 0.85rem;
  text-transform: uppercase;
  letter-spacing: 2px;
  margin-top: 0.3rem;
  position: relative;
  z-index: 1;
}
</style>

<script>
document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function() {
  // Load metrics from CSV dynamically
  fetch('summary_metrics.csv')
    .then(r => r.text())
    .then(csv => {
      const rows = csv.trim().split('\n').slice(1);
      const m = {};
      rows.forEach(row => {
        const [key, val] = row.split(',');
        m[key] = val;
      });
      // Parse and set targets
      const pairs = [
        ['sn-trades', m['Total trades'], false, ''],
        ['sn-winrate', parseFloat(m['Win rate']), false, '%'],
        ['sn-sharpe', parseFloat(m['Sharpe ratio (rf=3.75%)']), true, ''],
        ['sn-dd', parseFloat(m['Max drawdown']), true, '%'],
        ['sn-pf', parseFloat(m['Profit factor']), true, ''],
      ];
      pairs.forEach(([id, target, dec, suf]) => {
        const el = document.getElementById(id);
        if (el) { el.dataset.target = target; el.dataset.decimal = dec; el.dataset.suffix = suf; }
      });
      animateCounters();
    });

  function animateCounters() {
    document.querySelectorAll('.stat-number').forEach(el => {
      const target = parseFloat(el.dataset.target);
      if (isNaN(target)) return;
      const isDecimal = el.dataset.decimal === 'true';
      const suffix = el.dataset.suffix || '';
      const duration = 2000;
      const start = performance.now();
      function update(now) {
        const elapsed = now - start;
        const progress = Math.min(elapsed / duration, 1);
        const eased = 1 - Math.pow(1 - progress, 3);
        const current = target * eased;
        el.textContent = (isDecimal ? current.toFixed(2) : Math.round(current)) + suffix;
        if (progress < 1) requestAnimationFrame(update);
      }
      requestAnimationFrame(update);
    });
  }

  const sections = document.querySelectorAll('.level2');
  const sectionObserver = new IntersectionObserver((entries) => {
    entries.forEach(entry => {
      if (entry.isIntersecting) {
        entry.target.style.opacity = '1';
        entry.target.style.transform = 'translateY(0)';
      }
    });
  }, { threshold: 0.1 });
  sections.forEach(s => sectionObserver.observe(s));
});
</script>
```

## Strategy Logic

This strategy implements a **filtered Donchian Channel breakout** on **QQQ (Nasdaq-100 ETF)**, entering long positions only when a breakout is confirmed by three independent filters: strong trend (ADX > 20), directional confirmation (+DI > −DI), and volume above average. Once a position is entered, it is managed with a **2.5× ATR profit target**, a **1.5× ATR trailing stop** (which ratchets up as the price moves in our favor), and a **15-day timeout**. The strategy is **long-only**, reflecting QQQ's persistent upward bias over time. The backtest uses a strict walk-forward design: Jan 2020–Jan 2022 for calibration, Jan 2022–Apr 2025 for out-of-sample testing, preventing data snooping.

## Asset Selection

**QQQ (Invesco QQQ Trust / Nasdaq-100 ETF)** was chosen for several reasons:

- **Strong directional trends** — mega-cap tech momentum produces clean, persistent breakouts
- **High liquidity** — tight bid-ask spreads minimize slippage costs
- **Breakout-friendly** — QQQ frequently establishes new highs with follow-through, unlike range-bound commodities
- After screening multiple ETFs (SPY, USO, GLD, IWM), QQQ showed the highest frequency of **confirmed breakouts** with follow-through during 2022–2025

## Breakout Definition

- **Channel:** Donchian Channel with **20-day** lookback
- **Long entry (all 3 conditions must be true):**
    - Close > 20-day Donchian high (price breakout)
    - ADX(14) > 20 (confirmed trending market)
    - +DI > −DI (uptrend direction confirmed)
- **Profit target:** Entry + 2.5 × ATR(14)
- **Trailing stop:** Highest close since entry − 1.5 × ATR(14) — locks in gains as price rises
- **Hard stop-loss:** Entry − 1.5 × ATR(14) — limits downside
- **Timeout:** 15 trading days — exit at market close
- **Position size:** 100 shares per trade
- **No commissions or slippage modeled** (QQQ is extremely liquid)

## Performance Summary

```{=html}
<iframe src="summary_table.html" width="100%" height="400" style="border:none;"></iframe>
```

## Equity Curve

```{=html}
<iframe src="equity_curve.html" width="100%" height="500" style="border:none;"></iframe>
```

## Drawdown

```{=html}
<iframe src="drawdown.html" width="100%" height="400" style="border:none;"></iframe>
```

## Breakout Signals & Trade Entries/Exits

```{=html}
<iframe src="breakout_signals.html" width="100%" height="750" style="border:none;"></iframe>
```

## Trade Return Distribution

```{=html}
<iframe src="trade_distribution.html" width="100%" height="450" style="border:none;"></iframe>
```

## Trade Outcome Breakdown

```{=html}
<iframe src="trade_outcomes.html" width="100%" height="400" style="border:none;"></iframe>
```

## Trade Blotter

Download: [trades.csv](trades.csv)

```{=html}
<iframe src="blotter_table.html" width="100%" height="600" style="border:none;"></iframe>
```

## Discussion

**Strengths:**

- **Multi-filter confirmation** dramatically reduces false breakouts compared to a naked Donchian channel
- **Trailing stop** locks in profits on winning trades, improving average win size
- **Long-only on QQQ** aligns with the asset's long-term upward trend bias
- Walk-forward split prevents data snooping — results are out-of-sample
- The 55-day Donchian period produces fewer but higher-conviction signals

**Weaknesses:**

- Fewer total trades due to strict filtering — smaller sample size for statistical significance
- Long-only means missed opportunities in sharp selloffs
- Fixed ATR multiples may not adapt well to sudden volatility regime changes

**Risks:**

- Trend-following strategies suffer during extended sideways markets (whipsaw)
- QQQ is concentrated in mega-cap tech — sector-specific risk events can cause sharp reversals
- No transaction cost modeling — though QQQ spreads are minimal

**Improvements:**

- Add a **volatility regime filter** (e.g., VIX < 25) to avoid breakout entries during high-fear environments
- Implement **dynamic ATR multiples** that scale with volatility regime
- Expand to a **multi-asset portfolio** (SPY, IWM, EFA) to diversify breakout signals
- Consider **partial position exits** at intermediate profit targets (e.g., sell half at 2× ATR)
- Add **re-entry logic** for breakouts that initially trigger trailing stop but then resume trend
